title: Podcast | Is the future looking bright or bleak for European workers?
content: Can the EU become more competitive while preserving its workers’ rights? What role do trade unions have in a time of deregulation? Is AI going to replace Europeans’ jobs? Brussels, My Love? tries to answer these questions.

title: Activists on Gaza flotilla intercepted by Israel disembark in Crete
content: Several European governments with nationals among those arrested have called on Israel to free the activists and called its action a flagrant contravention of international law, while Washington backed Israeli authorities, calling the flotilla a "stunt".

title: Man charged with attempted murder over stabbings of Jewish men in London
content: The suspect, identified as Essa Suleiman, faces three counts of attempted murder following Wednesday’s stabbings of two Jewish men, as well as another incident earlier elsewhere in London.
snippet: The suspect, identified as Essa Suleiman, faces three counts of attempted murder following Wednesday’s stabbings of two Jewish men, as well as another incident earlier elsewhere in London.

title: After Orbán's defeat, the EU faces tough questions about Ukraine's accession
content: The imminent departure of Viktor Orbán is forcing EU leaders to confront uncomfortable questions about Ukraine's accession process that the Hungarian veto had, until now, kept unanswered.
snippet: The imminent departure of Viktor Orbán is forcing EU leaders to confront uncomfortable questions about Ukraine's accession process that the Hungarian veto had, until now, kept unanswered.

title: UK raises terror threat level to severe after two Jewish men stabbed in north London
content: Severe is the second-highest rung on a five-point scale and means intelligence agencies consider an attack highly likely in the next six months.
snippet: Severe is the second-highest rung on a five-point scale and means intelligence agencies consider an attack highly likely in the next six months.

title: Trump launches fresh tirade against Merz after troop withdrawal threat
content: Trump's barrage of criticism comes in response to Merz's comments on Monday that the US was being "humiliated" by the Iranian leadership.
snippet: Trump's barrage of criticism comes in response to Merz's comments on Monday that the US was being "humiliated" by the Iranian leadership.

title: 'We are ready to discuss digital rules with the US but cannot wipe out rules,' EU trade chief says
content: After months of pressure from the US, EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič told Euronews that Brussels has agreed to open a “digital dialogue” with Washington. Still, US tariffs on EU steel and aluminium remain.

title: EU shouldn't 'humiliate' itself by seeking direct talks with Russia, warns Kallas
content: High Representative Kaja Kallas has once again rebuffed the idea of direct talks between the European Union and Russia, warning that Moscow is "gearing up its military for a long-term confrontation with the West".

title: French teen charged in Singapore over vending machine straw-licking viral video
content: Singapore, a small, densely populated city-state in southeast Asia, tightly regulates public behaviour and cleanliness.
snippet: Singapore, a small, densely populated city-state in southeast Asia, tightly regulates public behaviour and cleanliness.

title: Spain's PM 'was at level one' in scheme to rig public contracts, defendant in graft case says
content: The Socialists' turmoil has rocked relations with their far-left junior coalition partner Sumar and an array of fringe and regional separatist parties whose support is essential to pass legislation.
snippet: The Socialists' turmoil has rocked relations with their far-left junior coalition partner Sumar and an array of fringe and regional separatist parties whose support is essential to pass legislation.

title: China pushes EU capitals to scrap 'Made in Europe' law or face retaliation
content: As EU countries debate the European Commission’s proposal, Beijing is urging national governments to abandon the planned law. Otherwise, China says it will take countermeasures.
snippet: As EU countries debate the European Commission’s proposal, Beijing is urging national governments to abandon the planned law. Otherwise, China says it will take countermeasures.

title: Italy says it has filed to be civil party in probe into Swiss bar fire that killed 41
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has vowed justice for Italian victims while Italy has opened its own investigation into the fire even as the Swiss probe continues.
snippet: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has vowed justice for Italian victims while Italy has opened its own investigation into the fire even as the Swiss probe continues.

title: Magyar says EU money to arrive in Hungary 'soon' after meeting with von der Leyen
content: Hungary’s incoming prime minister hailed "productive" meetings in Brussels as he races to unlock billions in EU funding ahead of a summer deadline. Commission President von der Leyen vows to keep working together.

title: Europol GRIMM taskforce nets 280 arrests as 'violence for hire' spreads across Europe
content: The taskforce sees Europol collaborate with police forces in 11 countries and has already identified more than 1,400 people linked to violence as a service.
snippet: The taskforce sees Europol collaborate with police forces in 11 countries and has already identified more than 1,400 people linked to violence as a service.

title: Workers' health isn't the only thing at risk of job strain — the economy is too, study finds
content: As Europe marks Labour Day, work strain is taking a deadly toll on employees worldwide. From burnout to anxiety, many Europeans say they dare not speak out about their concerns for fear of negative consequences.

title: Dutch football league passport dispute could force replay of 133 matches
content: An appeal lodged by NAC Breda over the status of players who chose to play for nations other than the Netherlands could bring this season's Eredivisie to a halt and force a repeat of 133 matches in the worst-case scenario.

title: Fact check: Did Ukraine's US ambassador steal champagne during White House dinner shooting?
content: Social media users have misleadingly claimed that viral footage shows Ukraine's ambassador to the US stealing bottles of champagne from vacant tables, amid the chaos following the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

title: UK PM Starmer jeered by crowd on arrival at scene of stabbing of two Jewish men
content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is heckled with jeers of "Jew harmer" and "shame on you" as he arrives in north London where two Jewish men were stabbed the day before. The attack, which the police have labelled a "terrorist incident", comes after a series of attacks on Jewish sites in the area.

title: Spain's 'humane migration policy' is 'model for Europe', minister says
content: Spain's Migration Minister defended the country’s migration approach in an interview with Euronews amid criticism from EU leaders on plans to regularise an estimated 500,000 undocumented migrants.
snippet: Spain's Migration Minister defended the country’s migration approach in an interview with Euronews amid criticism from EU leaders on plans to regularise an estimated 500,000 undocumented migrants.

title: Exclusive: EU vows to fight ‘tooth and nail’ for European industry as China threatens retaliation
content: In an interview with Euronews, EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič said the European Union would stand its ground after China threatened retaliatory measures over plans to strengthen the bloc’s industrial policy.

title: Belgium reopens the nuclear door in high-stakes deal with Engie
content: The business venture aims to reach an agreement on the takeover of the nuclear fleet by 1 October, but it's unclear how much Belgium will pay for this nuclear renaissance.
snippet: The business venture aims to reach an agreement on the takeover of the nuclear fleet by 1 October, but it's unclear how much Belgium will pay for this nuclear renaissance.

title: Croatia signs US-backed deal to expand Balkan gas infrastructure amid EU climate concerns

title: Belgium seeks to take control of nuclear reactors in move to secure energy supplies
content: Brussels believes the solution to soaring energy prices as a result of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine and the war in the Middle East is to permanently transition away from fossil fuels.
snippet: Brussels believes the solution to soaring energy prices as a result of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine and the war in the Middle East is to permanently transition away from fossil fuels.

title: Royal recruits boost volunteers as the Netherlands builds up its military reservists
content: The royal seal of approval, together with recruitment campaigns running across the media, has proven so successful that the military is now working overtime to arm, train and accommodate the influx of newcomers.

title: Kyiv shuns Moscow's 'Victory Day truce' asking for long-term ceasefire
content: After the Easter truce, Moscow is now proposing a Victory Day parade ceasefire as the Kremlin is concerned over Ukraine’s long-range striking capabilities. Moscow has already scaled down the parade, where for the first time in years, Russian armoured columns will not be rolling on the Red Square.

title: Israel intercepts Gaza-bound flotilla, arresting 175 activists

title: Israel halts Russian grain ship amid stolen Ukrainian wheat row
content: After days of diplomatic row between Ukraine and Israel, Israeli grain importer Zenziper said it is postponing the unloading of the grain on board the ship said to be carrying stolen Ukrainian grain on behalf of Russia.

title: Global press freedom at lowest level in 25 years, Reporters Without Borders report shows
content: In its latest report, RSF has warned that press freedom is at a 25-year low, with over half the world in “difficult” or “very serious” status and few “good” countries.
snippet: In its latest report, RSF has warned that press freedom is at a 25-year low, with over half the world in “difficult” or “very serious” status and few “good” countries.

title: The "giant" heads home: After war missions and Maduro capture, a record return
content: The world’s largest aircraft carrier is set to return to the US after completing missions that included operations in the Iran war and the US mission in Venezuela that saw the capture of Nicolas Maduro.
snippet: The world’s largest aircraft carrier is set to return to the US after completing missions that included operations in the Iran war and the US mission in Venezuela that saw the capture of Nicolas Madur

title: PSG vs Bayern - a semifinal for the ages and football at its very best
content: In this special football edition of The Ring, we deconstruct a Champions League game that will go down in history as one of the best ever. Nine goals - can top quality football really be any better? Our experts also weigh in on what else is moving Europe's most popular sport.

title: Switzerland or Italy? Who covers medical costs after the Crans-Montana fire?
content: After the deadly New Year’s Eve fire in the Swiss resort of Crans-Montana, tensions are rising after Switzerland sent invoices to the victims' families. What’s behind the bills?
snippet: After the deadly New Year’s Eve fire in the Swiss resort of Crans-Montana, tensions are rising after Switzerland sent invoices to the victims' families. What’s behind the bills?

title: EU failed to 'listen' in Sahel, envoy admits as influence wanes
content: EU envoy admits Brussels misread Sahel dynamics, warning that military-first strategies and one-way diplomacy have eroded influence as Russia and China deepen their foothold in the region.
snippet: EU envoy admits Brussels misread Sahel dynamics, warning that military-first strategies and one-way diplomacy have eroded influence as Russia and China deepen their foothold in the region.

title: 'We cannot give Russia legitimacy through sports events', says MEP Ville Niinistö
content: Ville Niinistö, MEP for the Greens, believes that Russia is now "weaker than a year ago" and says the country is using its foreign connections to "legitimise their war crimes in Ukraine".
snippet: Ville Niinistö, MEP for the Greens, believes that Russia is now "weaker than a year ago" and says the country is using its foreign connections to "legitimise their war crimes in Ukraine".

title: From statements to actions: Here's which European countries are spending the most on defence
content: Military spending by European NATO members last year rose faster than at any time since 1953.
snippet: Military spending by European NATO members last year rose faster than at any time since 1953.

title: Italian woman faces jail in Egypt for adultery as custody battle intensifies
content: Sentenced to six months in prison for adultery in Egypt, Nessy Guerra is caught up in a complex case of alleged abuse, a daughter stranded in the country and the limits of Italian intervention.
snippet: Sentenced to six months in prison for adultery in Egypt, Nessy Guerra is caught up in a complex case of alleged abuse, a daughter stranded in the country and the limits of Italian intervention.

title: Detained Gaza flotilla activists to be taken to Greece, Israel's foreign minister says

title: Europe Today: Péter Magyar first visit to Brussels and China-EU tensions rising

title: Czechia: A small country with big nuclear energy ambitions
content: Czechia stands out in Europe for its bold pursuit of nuclear energy sovereignty and its ambition to lead in atomic power as it cuts reliance on fossil fuels.
snippet: Czechia stands out in Europe for its bold pursuit of nuclear energy sovereignty and its ambition to lead in atomic power as it cuts reliance on fossil fuels.

title: Sweden confiscates false-flagged Russian 'shadow fleet' ship, prosecutors say
content: Russia has reportedly built up a flotilla of old oil tankers of opaque ownership to get around sanctions imposed by the European Union.

title: MEPs with new-borns can delegate their vote – but only if they are women
content: The European Parliament has approved a rule allowing proxy voting for new mothers before and after childbirth. However, it raises questions about gender equality, as it does not cover male MEPs.
snippet: The European Parliament has approved a rule allowing proxy voting for new mothers before and after childbirth. However, it raises questions about gender equality, as it does not cover male MEPs.

title: Second US ambassador to Ukraine steps down in less than one year
content: The acting US ambassador to Ukraine is to step down from her post in Kyiv and retire, the US State Department announced. Reports have indicated her reasons for leaving the job are related to the Trump administration's approach to Ukraine.

title: EU loosens state aid rules as Middle East crisis hits energy costs
content: Brussels loosens state aid rules as Middle East turmoil drives up fuel and fertiliser costs, allowing governments to cushion farmers, hauliers and industry with emergency subsidies.
snippet: Brussels loosens state aid rules as Middle East turmoil drives up fuel and fertiliser costs, allowing governments to cushion farmers, hauliers and industry with emergency subsidies.

title: Ukraine formally asks Israel to seize vessel carrying grain stolen by Russia

title: HAYI group claims responsibility for stabbing two Jewish men in north London
content: The incident comes in the wake of a spate of arson attacks on synagogues and Jewish community sites in north London in recent weeks.

title: Man accused of spying for Russia arrested in Berlin, prosecutors say
content: The suspect allegedly gave his Russian intelligence handler information about German military aid for Ukraine and photos of NATO military convoys moving along motorways.

title: Kubilius pushes Europen Defence Union but critics brand it a distraction
content: EU defence chief Andrius Kubilius is pushing a European Defence Union to tie Ukraine with its European allies, but the idea is already under fire, with one MEP warning that it risks distracting from the current job of bulletproofing the EU.

title: 'The EU is not the little sister of the US,' European Parliament Vice-President Wilmès says
content: The EU and the US should talk to each other as equal partners, European Parliament Vice-President and former Belgian Prime Minister Sophie Wilmès told Euronews' morning show Europe Today.
snippet: The EU and the US should talk to each other as equal partners, European Parliament Vice-President and former Belgian Prime Minister Sophie Wilmès told Euronews' morning show Europe Today.

title: EU backs ending new oil and gas drilling
content: The war in the Middle East may have driven up oil and gas demand, but the world should still ditch new fossil fuel drilling, EU climate chief Wopke Hoekstra has said, insisting on "affordable, reliable, homegrown clean energy."

title: How engaged are young Europeans in online civic and political life?
